title: --batch picks side-branch commit as anchor when batching mixed-branch work
description:
Identified as the root cause of Laura's v0.22.0 friction (osprey-strike, 2026-05-21). Her summary: 'My staged entry correctly covers all 3 README commits but it's anchored to f248f20f, a commit on the merged-in side branch ... --batch mode broke timbers' linear anchor model by anchoring an entry to a side-branch commit.'

FAILURE MODE
cmd/timbers/log_batch.go:228:
  anchor := group.commits[0].SHA

When --batch groups commits by Work-item trailer, all commits sharing a trailer go into one group. If that group includes both local-branch commits and merged-in side-branch commits (the cross-agent debt case), commits[0] can land on a side-branch SHA depending on slice ordering. The resulting entry is structurally valid but its anchor_commit references a side-branch commit, which then breaks all the linear-anchor assumptions downstream.

FIX
Replace commits[0].SHA with a topology-aware selection: prefer the newest commit on HEAD's first-parent line; fall back to commits[0] only when no commit in the group is on the first-parent line (pure cross-agent debt case — pending-detection will diagnose via the off-first-parent-line warning from v0.22.1).

Implementation sketch:
  func pickBatchAnchor(commits []git.Commit, head string) string {
    for _, c := range commits { // commits are walk-ordered, newest first
      if git.IsOnFirstParentLine(c.SHA, head) {
        return c.SHA
      }
    }
    return commits[0].SHA // pure-cross-agent fallback
  }

ACCEPTANCE
- New unit test: batch group containing one local + one side-branch commit → anchor lands on the local commit, not side-branch
- New unit test: batch group containing only side-branch commits → anchor falls back to commits[0] (graceful degradation; the off-first-parent diagnostic surfaces the situation)
- Existing batch tests pass (linear case unchanged)
- New integration test: --batch run with mixed-branch commits produces an entry whose anchor IS on HEAD's first-parent line

SIZE
XS — one helper function plus tests.

VERSION
v0.22.2
close_reason: Shipped in v0.22.2. Together with the direct docSet membership filter (incidentally fixed in the same patch), Laura's full friction class is resolved without algorithm-level changes.

title: Gate fallback when latest.anchor is reachable but off-first-parent
description:
When latest entry's anchor is reachable from HEAD via the DAG (so not stale-anchor) but NOT on HEAD's first-parent line, the gate's LogFirstParent(anchor, HEAD) walk produces structurally weird ranges — commits the user didn't expect because the exclude side is interpreted via full reachability while the include side is first-parent only.

CURRENT FAILURE MODE
Identified by second reviewer (2026-05-21). When the latest entry came in via a merge from a side branch (Laura's case), the gate walks LogFirstParent(side-branch-anchor, HEAD). The result includes commits on HEAD's first-parent line that aren't reachable from the side-branch anchor — i.e., commits on main before the merge — which are then filtered by docSet. Most of the time docSet covers them. But the ordering is opaque, and any docSet gap (the typical case being SHAs rewritten by rebase) leaks through as false-positive pending.

FIX (one-liner change in scope)
In internal/ledger/storage.go getPendingCommits, after the existing IsAncestorOf check, additionally check IsOnFirstParentLine when firstParent=true (gate path). If the anchor isn't on the first-parent line, route through the all-reachable + docSet path that the stale-anchor branch already uses. Do NOT wrap as ErrStaleAnchor — this isn't stale, just off-line.

ACCEPTANCE
- New unit test: anchor reachable + off-first-parent + gate path → uses all-reachable fallback
- Existing tests pass (linear case unchanged)
- Existing TestBranchMerge_SideBranchEntryWinsTimestamp still passes (display path unchanged)
- Display path (firstParent=false) keeps using full DAG Log(anchor, head) — already correct for this case

SIZE
XS — one branch in one if statement plus a focused unit test.

VERSION
v0.22.2
close_reason: Shipped in v0.22.2. Together with the direct docSet membership filter (incidentally fixed in the same patch), Laura's full friction class is resolved without algorithm-level changes.

title: Phase 0: diagnose side-branch latest-anchor + missing test for Laura's pathology
description:
Diagnostic-only fix for the v0.22.0 pending-detection friction Laura hit. No algorithm change; pure surface improvement plus a regression test that locks in the contract for any future algorithm work.

BACKGROUND
Laura on v0.22.0 hit a confusing pending state when PR #204 merged in entries whose anchors were on a side branch. The latest entry (by created_at) had its anchor on the side branch; IsAncestorOf returned true via merge reachability so the stale-anchor fallback didn't fire; the resulting Log(anchor, HEAD) walk produced structurally weird output. She eventually resolved via the existing TIMBERS_SKIP_CROSS_AGENT_DEBT=1 escape hatch, but only after a long back-and-forth fighting the model.

WHAT TO BUILD

1. Detection helper: latestAnchorIsOffFirstParentLine(entries, head) — runs the first-parent set from HEAD, checks whether the latest entry's anchor is in it. O(commits-on-first-parent) once, O(1) check.

2. Wire into timbers pending output:
   - Human mode: stderr warning when detection fires, naming the situation and the three escape hatches (TIMBERS_SKIP_CROSS_AGENT_DEBT=1, timbers ack <sha>, re-log on main).
   - JSON mode: new field 'latest_entry.anchor_off_first_parent_line: true' so downstream tooling can detect.

3. Wire into timbers doctor as a new check with full explanation.

4. New integration test in internal/integration/branch_merge_test.go:
   - Setup: main has commit + entry1 (anchored on main). Feature branch has commit + entry2 (anchored on feature). entry2.created_at > entry1.created_at (later by timestamp).
   - Merge feature to main.
   - Run timbers pending; assert behavior. This is the regression contract — codifies what 'correct' looks like for Laura's exact pathology so any future algorithm change is judged against it.

ACCEPTANCE
- Detection helper returns true in the Laura scenario, false in linear-history scenarios
- pending output (human + JSON) surfaces the hint when applicable
- doctor reports the situation with full explanation
- The new integration test passes with current behavior captured (we document what we observe; the algorithm change in Phase 1 will then update the assertion)
- No regressions in existing storage_test.go or branch_merge_test.go

SIZE
XS — diagnostic surface + one test. Algorithm unchanged.

VERSION
v0.22.1

title: display: drop clean merge commits from timbers pending (mirror gate's dropEmptyFileChanges)
description:
osprey-strike maintainer feedback: their pending list shows merge commits like 'Merge pull request #N' (203be479, eb566051) which have no content of their own — content lives in their parents. Gate already drops these via dropEmptyFileChanges (v0.21.0) but display path still walks full DAG and shows them.

Mirror the gate behavior in the display path:
- timbers pending, prime, status, catchup all use GetPendingCommits (full DAG)
- Drop merge commits (parents.length >= 2) with empty first-parent file changes from this output
- Resolution-merges that touched code on this branch's line still appear (correctly — those are real content)

This is narrower than 'auto-skip all merges' — preserves info on conflict-resolution merges that introduced code. Internally consistent with v0.21.0's gate filter.

IMPLEMENTATION
- Likely: lift dropEmptyFileChanges (currently gateStrict-only) to apply when commit has 2+ parents, in both gate and display paths
- Need a way to count parents per commit — extend Commit struct or query git
- Audit-test: ensure single-parent commits with empty file lists (shouldn't exist outside --allow-empty) are NOT dropped from display

ACCEPTANCE
- Clean PR merges no longer appear in timbers pending output
- Merges with first-parent file changes (conflict resolution touched code) still appear
- Display and gate now agree on merge handling
- Existing tests pass; new tests cover both branches
close_reason: GetPendingCommits now drops empty-file merge commits via dropEmptyMerges. Single-parent empty commits stay visible. Gate keeps the stricter dropEmptyFileChanges. Tests cover clean PR merges, conflict-resolution merges, --allow-empty markers.

title: prime: make commit → log → push order explicit; warn in timbers log when origin is ahead of HEAD
description:
Push-before-log race in osprey-strike report: agent committed code, pushed, then ran timbers log. Entry file was created and auto-committed locally but never pushed. PR merged on GitHub, content commit landed on main with orphaned entry only on agent's local.

Two complementary fixes:

1. timbers prime injection: make the order explicit (currently 'commit → log' which permits 'commit → push → log' literal reading). Reword to 'commit code → timbers log (writes entry, auto-commits) → git push (sends content + entry together)'.

2. timbers log warning: when the working tree is clean AND origin/<branch> is ahead of HEAD (we pushed before logging), print a warning to stderr noting the entry is now stranded locally and the push needs to happen again to ship it.

ACCEPTANCE
- timbers prime output explicitly orders the three steps in injected snippet
- timbers log detects ahead-of-origin state post-commit and warns
- Existing tests pass; new tests cover the ahead-of-origin warn path

SIZE
XS
close_reason: prime/MCP protocol text now explicitly orders commit→log→push with a callout that pushing between is an error. timbers log warns when the documented commit is already on upstream but the entry's auto-commit isn't (push-before-log race). IsPushedToUpstream helper + unit tests in internal/git.

title: Fix post-commit hook nudging on infrastructure-only commits
description: In v0.20.0, timbers hook run post-commit prints the document-this-commit reminder unconditionally, ignoring built-in skip rules and .timbersignore. timbers pending and timbers log correctly recognize that .beads/*-only (or other skipped-path-only) commits are not actionable, but the post-commit hook nudges anyway. Mirror the pre-commit hook's HasPendingCommits gate inside runPostCommitHook so the reminder only fires when there is actionable undocumented work. Add regression tests covering .beads/issues.jsonl-only and .timbersignore-skipped commits.
close_reason: Fixed in commit c5e7a8a. Post-commit hook now mirrors pre-commit gating via shared hasActionablePending() helper. Regression tests in cmd/timbers/hook_run_test.go cover .beads/, .timbers/, lockfile, .timbersignore-skipped, substantive, and missing-.timbers/ cases plus pre-commit parity.

title: Fix go install: rename ledger files to remove colons
description: Colons in .timbers/*.json filenames break Go's module zip format and the proxy, blocking 'go install github.com/gorewood/timbers/cmd/timbers@latest' for all v0.16.x and v0.17.x tags. Filenames are internal — the JSON 'id' field keeps the canonical ISO 8601 timestamp. Replace ':' with '-' in filenames only. Must remain backward compatible: readers accept both old (colon) and new (dash) filename formats indefinitely so existing forks/clones with old entries keep working. Writers always emit the new format. Migration command renames in-place under .timbers/. Run migration on this repo, tag v0.18.0.
acceptance_criteria: readers parse both old and new filename formats; writers emit dash-only filenames; existing colon files in any clone remain readable; new entries land with dash filenames; doctor or migration command renames colon files in .timbers/; this repo's .timbers/ is migrated; v0.18.0 installs via 'go install ...@latest' without error; tests cover both filename formats round-trip
close_reason: Closed

title: Detect stale anchor via reachability, not just existence
description: After rebase/squash-merge, old anchor SHAs linger in the object store for ~2 weeks. git log anchor..HEAD succeeds but returns phantom commits. Add git merge-base --is-ancestor check before git log to detect unreachable anchors.
close_reason: Added IsAncestorOf reachability check before git log in GetPendingCommits

title: Fix doctor --fix scope mismatch for stale hook cleanup
description: doctor --fix hardcodes Install(true) (project-local) when cleaning stale hooks, even if the stale hooks were detected in global settings. This leaves retired hooks like PreToolUse in place.
close_reason: Fixed scope mismatch - Install() now uses detected scope instead of hardcoded project-local

title: Stale anchor: pending shows all commits, confuses agents
description: On stale anchor (squash/rebase), GetPendingCommits falls back to all reachable commits. HasPendingCommits returns (false, ErrStaleAnchor) so hooks silently allow, but timbers pending shows hundreds of commits with a long warning. Agents see the list, try to re-document, create duplicates. Fix: pending should report 0 actionable on stale anchor with clear guidance.
close_reason: Closed

title: query --range misses entries after squash merge
description: getEntriesByRange matches entries by anchor_commit ancestry, but after squash-merge the original feature branch SHAs aren't in main's history. Entry files are present in the squash commit diff but query returns 0 results. Fix: fall back to git diff --name-only A..B -- .timbers/ when anchor-based matching returns nothing.
close_reason: Fallback to git diff --name-only for file-based entry discovery when anchor commits are unreachable

title: Document ack-for-rebase pattern (rebase-preserved-content SHAs)
description: When a rebase/force-push gives an already-documented commit a new SHA with unchanged content AND the old anchor is still reachable, pending flags the new SHA and it does NOT self-heal (unlike stale-anchor squash case). Today the honest, low-friction fix is 'timbers ack <new-sha> --reason "rebased; content in <entry-id>"' — a structured record that counts as documented. But this pattern is undocumented: StaleAnchorGuidance only covers the GC'd-anchor case, and the cross-branch/doctor ack hints use a generic '...' reason. Scope (step 1 of rebase-relink eval): add a RebaseRelinkGuidance protocol section (reaches prime + MCP), sharpen the pending and doctor ack hints to copy-pasteable rebase reasons, and add a DX-guide subsection. Steps 2 (typed ack --to) and 3 (patch-id gate) are deferred follow-ups.
close_reason: Shipped: RebaseRelinkGuidance protocol section + sharpened ack hints + DX-guide subsection. Steps 2/3 deferred.

title: timbers upgrade: detect install method and self-upgrade
description:
Provide a single command that upgrades the timbers binary regardless of how it was installed. Today users have to remember which install method they used and run the corresponding upgrade dance:

- install.sh users: re-run 'curl ... install.sh | bash'
- just install-release users: 'just install-release X.Y.Z' from the project
- go install users: discouraged but exists; 'go install github.com/gorewood/timbers/cmd/timbers@latest'
- Homebrew/other packaging: TBD

PROPOSAL
'timbers upgrade' (or 'timbers update' — pick one; 'upgrade' implies binary, more conventional)
- Detects the install method via heuristics:
  - Binary path location (e.g., ~/.local/bin/timbers => install.sh; /bin/timbers => go install; etc.)
  - Build metadata in 'timbers --version' (commit SHA, build date — release vs dev)
  - Filesystem checks (is install.sh's install marker present?)
- Executes the appropriate upgrade command
- For unsupported / unrecognized install paths: error out with explicit instructions (don't guess)
- Optional: --check flag to report 'X is installed, Y is latest, install method is Z' without performing the upgrade
- Optional: --dry-run flag

DETECTION HEURISTICS (sketch)
-  resolves the actual binary path
- If under ~/.local/bin/ AND ~/.local/share/timbers/install.log exists (or similar marker) => install.sh
- If under /Users/bob/.local/share/mise/installs/go/1.25.8/bin or /bin AND version says 'dev' => go install (warn: 'go install puts a versionless dev binary in GOBIN that shadows release binaries; recommend uninstalling and using install.sh')
- If under /usr/local/bin => possibly Homebrew or manual; check with 'brew list timbers' if brew present
- If under repo-local bin/ (e.g., /Users/bob/Projects/agent/timbers/bin/timbers from just install-local) => 'this is a dev build; use just install-local to refresh'
- Else => unrecognized; print the supported install methods and exit non-zero

LATEST-VERSION CHECK
Already partially possible: 'timbers --version' shows local version. To check 'is there a newer release?', query GitHub Releases API or just install.sh's own logic. Could share code with install.sh's resolution.

ACCEPTANCE
- 'timbers upgrade' works end-to-end for install.sh users (the most common case)
- Refuses to upgrade go install users; tells them how to migrate to install.sh
- Refuses to upgrade dev builds; tells the user 'this is a dev build, use just install-local from the repo'
- 'timbers upgrade --check' is non-destructive and shows version + install method + upgrade path
- 'timbers doctor' reports the install method as part of its Version check (currently shows 'latest' check only)

OPEN QUESTIONS
- Does install.sh leave a marker file we can rely on? If not, we may need to add one in install.sh itself (small change, easy).
- For users behind corporate proxies / air-gapped: detection should degrade gracefully when GitHub Releases API isn't reachable.

SIZE
S — most of the logic is shell-out + path checks. The install.sh marker (if needed) is a one-liner in install.sh.

VERSION
v0.23.x or later (queue after tree-equivalence docSet v0.24.0)

title: just release: monitor GH Action and auto-run install-release on success
description:
Today's manual workflow after 'just release X.Y.Z':
1. Wait (uncertain duration) for GH Actions release-build workflow to finish
2. Remember to run 'just install-release' to get the binary on this machine
3. (Often forgotten; user ends up on a stale local binary until next release)

PROPOSAL
Extend 'just release' (or add 'just release-and-install') to:
1. Push tag + main as today
2. Wait for the GH Actions release workflow to start (poll 'gh run list' or similar)
3. Monitor that run until it completes (success/failure)
4. On success: run 'just install-release' automatically
5. On failure or timeout (e.g., 10 min): report and exit non-zero
6. The monitor uses 'gh run watch' if it exists, otherwise a poll loop

ACCEPTANCE
- After 'just release X.Y.Z' completes, local machine has the X.Y.Z binary
- If GH Actions fails, the recipe exits non-zero with a useful message
- Reasonable timeout (configurable via env or arg)
- Doesn't break existing 'just release' for users who don't want the auto-install (keep as separate recipe, or as a flag)

RELATED
Bob: 'we set up a monitor for the gh action that actually makes the release available to complete, then run our own just install-release recipe. this would make sure our machine always ends up with latest rather than me having to remember to manually install from the gh release.'

SIZE
S — justfile change + maybe a small shell helper. No Go code changes needed.

VERSION
v0.23.0 or follow-up patch

title: onboarding: timbers setup claude missing from osprey-strike README
description:
Laura's v0.22.1 timbers doctor flagged 'Claude Code Integration: Claude Code hooks not configured for timbers' because she followed osprey-strike's README during onboarding, which doesn't mention 'timbers setup claude'.

ROOT CAUSE
osprey-strike README Quick Start (lines 1-60) covers:
- mise install
- beads install
- timbers install
- mise install (tools)
- direnv setup
- just setup (project hooks + per-package deps)

It does NOT mention 'timbers setup claude'.

The README has a 'DO NOT run these' section (line 244-247) that explicitly warns against 'timbers hooks install' because the project uses shared hooks via core.hooksPath -> .githooks/. The named-similarly 'timbers setup claude' is a DIFFERENT command (installs Claude Code SessionStart hook into ~/.claude/settings.json or .claude/settings.json) and should be encouraged, not discouraged. Easy to confuse the two.

The osprey-strike AGENTS.md does mention it (line 507: 'Run timbers prime for workflow context, or install hooks (timbers setup claude) for auto-injection') but that's in a deep section new contributors are unlikely to read end-to-end.

EFFECT FOR USERS
- Project-level git hooks (in .githooks/) still fire — gate still works
- Per-session prime injection doesn't fire — Claude Code agent doesn't auto-receive 'pending commits', 'recent entries', and protocol context at session start
- Result: agent has to re-discover the protocol every session via fallback reasoning, increasing the chance of friction (e.g., Laura's session manually arriving at TIMBERS_SKIP_CROSS_AGENT_DEBT=1)

THIS IS A TIMBERS PROJECT ONBOARDING DOCS ISSUE, NOT A TIMBERS BUG
The osprey-strike repo's docs need updating, not timbers itself. But timbers could help by surfacing this in 'timbers init' / 'timbers onboard' outputs, and by clarifying the docs around the two similarly-named commands.

PROPOSED ACTIONS
1. Update timbers onboard output to clearly recommend 'timbers setup claude' as a separate step from any 'hooks install' guidance
2. Update timbers prime output to surface 'Claude Code hooks not installed; run timbers setup claude' as a session-start hint when applicable
3. Add a 'setup-vs-hooks-install' clarification to the timbers docs/onboarding guide
4. (Outside this bead) osprey-strike README update — file in their repo

SIZE
S — docs + a small detection helper. Defer until v0.23 or later.

VERSION
TBD

title: Tree-equivalence docSet: tolerate SHA rewriting via rebase/squash/cherry-pick
description:
Independent reviewer's durable-fix recommendation. docSet membership is currently keyed by exact SHA match — any rebase, squash, or cherry-pick rewrites SHAs and the docSet entry no longer matches the new commit, even though the *content* (tree, message, files) is identical. The result: false-positive pending after history rewriting.

This isn't just theoretical — squash-merges (GitHub UI default) rewrite SHAs every time a PR lands. Any entries logged on a feature branch pre-squash are still in the merged result but their workset.commits SHAs point to the GC'd pre-squash commits, not the squashed result.

PROPOSAL
Broaden docSet membership: for each documented SHA in any entry's workset.commits, also include any current commit reachable from HEAD whose *tree* matches the documented SHA's tree. Tree-equivalence is rebase/squash/cherry-pick-tolerant by construction.

Performance consideration: for each documented SHA, look up the tree via git cat-file (cheap, O(N entries) total). Then for each candidate pending commit, compare its tree against the docSet's tree-set. O(M pending × log N) with a set.

Alternative considered: content-hash matching (files + message). More robust but more code; tree-match handles the typical case (file changes preserved, message preserved or rewritten but commit is same work).

ACCEPTANCE
- After rebase that rewrites SHAs in HEAD's history, previously-documented commits stay covered by docSet
- After squash-merge, the squashed commit is considered covered by the original entries' tree-equivalence
- Cherry-picked commits with identical trees count as documented
- Performance benchmark: no regression on linear-history pending check (the common case)

OPEN QUESTIONS
- Where to compute tree-set: lazily in classifyCommit (per-call cost) or eagerly when building docSet (once per pending check)? Eagerly is likely better.
- Cache invalidation: tree-set is purely a function of entry SHAs + git state; no caching needed beyond what filterCommits already does within a single pending check.
- Edge case: entries whose workset.commits references a GC'd SHA — git cat-file fails. Treat as 'tree unknown' and degrade gracefully (no false-positive coverage; the original SHA match still works for non-GC'd commits).

SIZE
M — new helper, integration with existing filter chain, performance characterization. The tipping signal for prioritizing this is a second user report of post-rebase or post-squash false-positive pending.

VERSION
v0.24.0 (or later — gated on real-world evidence)

title: Phase 1: anchor-set-termination walk for pending detection
description:
Replace the single-anchor walk in getPendingCommits with a first-parent walk from HEAD backward, terminating when we hit any commit in the set of all entry anchors. Principled termination condition (no magic N); backward-compatible by construction for linear histories; resolves Laura-class topology bugs at the algorithm level.

BACKGROUND
Phase 0 (separate bead) adds a diagnostic surface but doesn't change behavior. This bead is the actual fix. Originally scoped as 'first-parent-aware latestEntry' but reviewer caught that approach as a regression for the solo-dev-feature-branch case (entry on feature branch → merge to main → main's first-parent line doesn't contain the entry's anchor → fallback to 'all reachable as pending' false-positives the dev's own work). The corrected design is anchor-SET termination, not anchor selection.

ALGORITHM
Replace s.git.Log(anchor, head) with a first-parent walk from HEAD:
1. Build anchorSet = {entry.workset.anchor_commit for entry in entries}
2. Walk first-parent from HEAD backward:
   - Collect each commit
   - Terminate when we hit a commit in anchorSet
   - Cap at 5000 commits to prevent pathological unbounded walks
3. Apply existing filter chain (docSet, ackedSet, skip rules, dropEmptyMerges) to the collected commits

WHY THIS IS RIGHT
- Linear history: first anchor hit is the latest entry's anchor → identical to current behavior. Backward-compatible.
- Solo dev creates entry on feature branch, merges to main: walk from main HEAD past the merge commit, past the initial commit's anchor → terminate. Feature-branch commits filtered by docSet. Pending correct.
- Laura's case: walk from PR-#204-merge HEAD, hit her main-line 522ebf anchor → terminate. Side-branch entry's anchor is never reached. Pending correct.
- No 'latest entry' selection. Cross-branch entries contribute to docSet but can't dominate the cursor.
- Termination is principled set-membership, not magic N.

TEST ADDITIONS
- Update the Phase 0 integration test assertion to reflect new behavior (Laura's pending is clean, not noisy)
- Solo-dev-feature-branch case: assert pending is clean after merge (no false-positive on documented feature commits)
- Walk-cap behavior: synthesize repo with 6000 undocumented commits, assert walk caps at 5000 with a 'capped' signal
- No-entries case: all reachable from HEAD (unchanged from current behavior)

GATED ON
- Phase 0 (timbers-???) shipped in v0.22.1
- 1 week empirical soak — see if Phase 0's diagnostic surface is sufficient or if the algorithm change is actually needed
- Phase 0's integration test passing — defines the correctness contract this change must satisfy

OPEN QUESTIONS (defer to implementation)
- Walk cap: is 5000 right? Lower is safer; higher accommodates catchup-territory repos. Probably make it a const, name it walkCapForPending, and let users override via env if it ever bites.
- Performance: O(commits-since-latest-on-first-parent-line). For typical use small. Benchmark before/after to confirm no regression on existing test corpus.

SIZE
M — algorithm change with new test surface. Lower risk than v0.21.0's first-parent gate change because Phase 0 locks in the correctness contract via the new integration test before any algorithm churn happens.

VERSION
v0.23.0

title: TIMBERS_DEBUG=1: surface gate's pending-detection decisions to stderr
description:
Diagnostic surface for 'why is the gate firing / not firing on this commit?' Exposed during the osprey-strike empirical-block investigation when TIMBERS_LOG=debug did nothing (we have no debug knob).

When TIMBERS_DEBUG=1 (or =true), hook gate and pending commands emit a single structured stderr line:

[timbers] gate: anchor=<short> head=<short> walk=<full-dag|first-parent> seen=<N> dropped=<N> (<reasons>) actionable=<N>

Where reasons is a comma-separated breakdown: 'infra=2,revert=1,empty-files=1,author-skip=0'.

This would have made the osprey-strike diagnostic a 30-second exercise: run hook with TIMBERS_DEBUG=1, see exactly what the gate saw and why each commit was kept/dropped.

ACCEPTANCE
- TIMBERS_DEBUG=1 enables the trace; unset/0/false disables (default)
- Output is single-line, parseable, goes to stderr (so it doesn't break --json)
- Covers both gate path (HasPendingCommits) and display path (GetPendingCommits)
- Existing tests pass; new test covers env-var on/off

SIZE
XS — small, defense-in-depth, useful for diagnosing future reports
close_reason: TIMBERS_DEBUG=1 traces per-commit classification (keep | skip <reason>) to stderr with a stable parseable format. Covers gate and display paths. Tests verify the trace fires when enabled and stays silent when unset.

title: timbers ack <SHA> --reason: decision-to-skip records (separate shape from log entries)
description:
osprey-strike report: when an agent (or human) hits a pending commit that doesn't merit a content entry, today's options are fabricate / --no-verify / hope-classifier-matches. None are honest. Add a first-class 'I saw this, doesn't need an entry, here's why' mechanism with audit trail.

DESIGN (spec first per pause-for-review cadence)

Command: timbers ack <SHA> --reason '<one-line>' [--scope mine|all]

Storage: .timbers/YYYY/MM/DD/ack_<SHA>.json — same git-tracked location as entries, different shape:
{
  schema: timbers.devlog/v1,
  kind: ack,  // NOT entry — pending-detection logic differentiates
  id: ack_<SHA>,
  acked_at: ISO8601,
  acker: git user.name / user.email,
  target_sha: <SHA>,
  reason: '<one-liner>',
  scope: mine|all  // mine = covers only my future pending checks; all = covers everyone
}

Pending-detection: an acked SHA counts as documented for the matched scope. Combined with infrastructure rules and revert auto-skip, this becomes a third bypass path with explicit audit trail.

Server-side use case: GH Action on pull_request: closed && merged == true runs 'timbers ack <merge_sha> --reason "GitHub merge of PR #N; content in entry tb_<id>" --scope all'. Self-healing merge-SHA gap, doesn't require client-side discipline. Once landed on main, ack flows to all contributors via normal git pull.

CLI surface:
- timbers ack <SHA> --reason '...' [--scope mine|all]
- timbers ack --list [--last N]
- timbers ack --remove <SHA> (rare, for mistakes)

ACCEPTANCE
- ack <SHA> writes ack record and stages it; SHA disappears from pending
- ack --scope mine only suppresses pending for the acker (matched by git user)
- ack --scope all suppresses for everyone
- ack record is human-readable JSON, git-tracked
- timbers pending --json includes 'acked_by' annotation when applicable
- timbers ack --list shows recent ack records with reasons

OPEN QUESTIONS
- Schema kind: 'ack' alongside 'entry' under same schema family
- Storage filename: ack_<SHA>.json or merged into a single acks.jsonl? Prefer per-file for git-merge friendliness
- Should ack count toward the anchor like entries do? Probably no — anchor is for 'work documented'; ack is for 'work intentionally skipped'

SIZE
M — new command, new schema shape, new storage path, integration with pending-detection. Spec-first per pause-for-review cadence; review pass required after landing.
close_reason: v1 ack command shipped: 'timbers ack <SHA> --reason' writes a decision-to-skip record under .timbers/YYYY/MM/DD/ack_*.json, kind='ack' alongside 'entry' in the same schema family. AckedSet integrated into filterByRules with reason 'ack' in classifyCommit and TIMBERS_DEBUG trace. Tests cover validation, JSON roundtrip, schema rejection of entry shape, storage roundtrip, and pending-filter integration. Deferred to follow-ups: ack --list, ack --remove, --scope mine, would_skip JSON annotation.

title: skip-authors: add author-glob skip rule for bot commits
description:
osprey-strike report: AI auto-review pipelines (q-redshifted opening autofix PRs) will produce content commits whose messages don't match the existing infra classifier (chore(beads):, chore(conduit):, build:). Each merged autofix PR adds pending content + pending merge to everyone's watermark.

Add a skip-by-author mechanism — composable with the existing message-prefix classifier, doesn't replace it.

DESIGN
- New file: .timbers/skip-authors.yml (or extend .timbersignore with [authors] section)
- Format: list of author globs, matched against commit.AuthorEmail (preferred) and commit.Author (fallback)
- Glob examples: 'q-redshifted', '*@bot.example.com', 'argocd-image-updater[bot]'
- Skip rule fires same way as infrastructure-only commits: dropped from both gate and display

API
- timbers config skip-authors list
- timbers config skip-authors add <glob>
- timbers config skip-authors remove <glob>
(or just have users hand-edit the yaml file; minimal CLI surface)

ACCEPTANCE
- Author-glob match drops the commit from both pending and the gate
- Test cases: exact match, glob wildcards, no-match (commit still pending), bad config file (warn + ignore, never break)
- Doctor command surfaces the loaded skip-authors when present
notes: Reopened: shipping refactor — fold author globs into .timbersignore via 'author:' line prefix instead of dedicated .timbers/skip-authors file. Reduces file sprawl, single source of truth for repo skip config.
close_reason: Refactored: author globs live in .timbersignore via 'author:' line prefix (per user feedback — no file sprawl). Single parse yields both path rules and author globs; matchesSkipAuthor stays in skipauthors.go as pure logic.

title: Gate: first-parent walk + TIMBERS_SKIP_CROSS_AGENT_DEBT env var
description:
Loosen pre/post-commit hook's pending check so cross-agent debt arriving via merges doesn't block independent agents on parallel worktrees.

PROBLEM
The gate currently calls storage.HasPendingCommits() -> GetPendingCommits() -> git.Log(anchor, HEAD), which walks the full DAG. When a sibling agent's commits land on main and the current branch merges main in, those commits become reachable from HEAD and surface in anchor..HEAD even though they were authored on another branch. Result: agent A is blocked on agent B's debt.

FIX (two complementary changes)
1. First-parent gate walk (the real fix): use 'git log --first-parent anchor..HEAD' in the gate-only pending path. Merged-in commits (reachable only via second parent) are silently excluded; in-branch debt still gates.
2. TIMBERS_SKIP_CROSS_AGENT_DEBT=1 escape hatch: env-var bypass in hasActionablePending(), parallel to the rebase/.timbers checks.

SCOPE
- Gate only (pre-commit, post-commit). Display commands (pending, prime, status, catchup) keep full-DAG view so debt awareness is preserved.

FILES
- internal/git/commit.go: extend Log or add LogFirstParent
- internal/ledger/storage.go: gate-specific pending path; HasPendingCommits uses it
- cmd/timbers/hook_run.go: env-var short-circuit in hasActionablePending
- internal/ledger/storage_test.go, cmd/timbers/hook_run_test.go, internal/integration/: tests

ACCEPTANCE
- Sibling-branch merge into current branch does not block 'git commit'
- In-branch undocumented commits still block
- TIMBERS_SKIP_CROSS_AGENT_DEBT=1 bypasses the gate
- 'timbers pending' unchanged (still shows full DAG)
- Stale-anchor path unchanged
close_reason: Re-closing — bd 1.0.x auto-flush still flaky on macOS. Both shipped: timbers-2t4 in v0.21.0, timbers-dws in v0.22.0.

2. TIMBERS_SKIP_CROSS_AGENT_DEBT=1 escape hatch: env-var bypass in hasActionablePending(), parallel to the rebase/.timbers checks. Cheap insurance for residual first-parent misses.

title: Document embedded-only beads/JSONL sync model
description: Drop the Dolt remote and update AGENTS.md to make the embedded JSONL transport the only sync channel. Stops agents from misreading auto-staged JSONL rewrites as corruption.

title: Fix prime compact mode follow-ups
description:
Follow-ups from review of timbers-8uo:
1. compactEntryID truncates IDs to tb_..._<sha> but timbers show cannot resolve that form — keep the full ID in compact output.
2. Compact mode silently drops .timbers/PRIME.md custom workflow content — show a hint when PRIME.md exists.
3. Health truncation uses 92 chars while entries use 96 — make consistent.
4. JSON mode always reports mode=compact v2 even with --full --json — set Mode from flags before JSON branch.
close_reason: Fixed all four follow-up issues in commit 3e986ed: full IDs in compact mode, PRIME.md hint, JSON mode honors --full, consistent health truncation. Quality gates green.

title: Review intentional local instruction and model updates
description: Review intentional local edits to AGENTS/CLAUDE docs and LLM model alias updates, fix correctness issues, run quality gates, and commit in logical units.
close_reason: Reviewed and committed instruction and model alias updates

title: Compact timbers prime output
description: Reduce default timbers prime output for session-start injection while preserving ledger workflow constraints. Keep full coaching available behind explicit full/guide mode and update hook wiring/tests.
close_reason: Implemented compact prime output

title: Relocate .timbersignore from .timbers/ to repo root
description: Move the per-repo skip-extension file from .timbers/.timbersignore to .timbersignore at the repo root, matching the convention of .gitignore, .dockerignore, .npmignore, etc.
design: Loader takes repo root instead of timbers dir. NewStorage gets the repo root via FileStorage (FileStorage.Dir is .timbers/, so derive parent) — no new constructor argument. Rename in tests.
acceptance_criteria: (1) .timbersignore at repo root is loaded and merged with defaults. (2) .timbers/.timbersignore no longer consulted. (3) All existing tests updated to write to repo root. (4) timbers status still reports skip count correctly. (5) just check passes.
close_reason: Closed

title: Configurable .timbersignore for skip path extension
description: Honor the comment in storage.go:254 anticipating .timbersignore. Allow per-repo extension of skip rules without code changes. Newline-delimited, # comments, supports prefix (trailing /) and suffix (leading *) matching.
design: Use .timbers/.timbersignore (newline-delimited), NOT config.toml — no new TOML dependency for one feature. Format: 'vendor/' (prefix), '*.lock' (suffix), 'third_party/'. Matcher loader in internal/ledger/. Merge with built-in defaults at storage init. No doublestar dependency — prefix + suffix only. Full glob support deferred until demand emerges.
acceptance_criteria: (1) .timbers/.timbersignore loaded if present. (2) Prefix entries (trailing /) match like current behavior. (3) Suffix entries (leading *) match by extension. (4) Comments and blank lines ignored. (5) Missing file is not an error. (6) Loader has unit tests with table-driven cases.
close_reason: Closed

title: Expand default infrastructurePrefixes for housekeeping commits
description: Auto-skip more housekeeping commits without config. Today only .timbers/ and .beads/ are skipped. Expand defaults to also cover: .gitignore, .gitattributes, .editorconfig, .github/dependabot.yml, .github/CODEOWNERS, .github/FUNDING.yml, .github/pull_request_template.md, renovate.json, dependabot.yml. Drop .github/ as a directory (too broad — workflows/ are substantive). Drop CHANGELOG.md (project-specific convention). Drop .claude/ (commands/skills can be substantive).
design: Fix latent prefix-match bug: entries without trailing / must require exact path match (today HasPrefix('.gitignores', '.gitignore') = true). Update isInfrastructureFile to distinguish 'directory prefix' (trailing /) from 'exact path' (no trailing /).
acceptance_criteria: (1) Default skip list expanded as listed. (2) Exact-path entries match exactly, not as prefixes. (3) Existing tests pass; new tests cover .gitignore vs .gitignores edge case. (4) Commits touching only .editorconfig auto-skip from pending.
close_reason: Closed

title: Make decision-log ADR numbering durable via {{vars.starting_number}}
description: decision-log template hardcodes 'starting from ADR-1' so every run renumbers, breaking ADRs as stable identifiers. Update template to use {{vars.starting_number}} with default 1. Document the caller-owned offset pattern (grep max ADR-N in target file, pass start+1 via --var). Add justfile recipe 'decision-log' that implements the pattern for the timbers repo's own decision log.
acceptance_criteria: template uses {{vars.starting_number}}; default of 1 preserves existing behavior; tutorial documents caller offset pattern; just decision-log recipe appends to docs/decisions.md with correct offset; integration test renders with explicit starting_number
close_reason: Closed

title: Add namespaced --var key=value flag to draft command
description: Templates can't accept caller-supplied variables. Add --var key=value (repeatable) to 'timbers draft', thread through RenderContext.Vars, and substitute as {{vars.key}} in buildVars. Namespacing under vars. keeps built-ins sacred and lets templates opt-in to caller input.
acceptance_criteria: --var key=value flag accepted (repeatable); {{vars.key}} substitutes in rendered output; unknown keys are silent no-ops; built-ins like {{entry_count}} not shadowed; unit tests cover merge behavior
close_reason: Closed

title: Pre-commit hook should skip in non-timbers worktrees
description: bd backup export-git uses a temp worktree on beads-backup branch. Git hooks are shared across worktrees, so the timbers pre-commit hook fires and may block backup commits. Timbers should detect it's not in a relevant context and get out of the way.
close_reason: Added explicit .timbers/ dir check in pre-commit hook for worktree compat

title: Document why separate commits are by design
description: Add design decision explaining separate ledger commits, surface via prime output and log --help. Addresses anticipated user concern about git log noise.
close_reason: Documented in docs/design-decisions.md, surfaced in prime output and log --help

title: Doctor: add stale anchor and merge strategy health checks
description: Doctor should detect stale anchor state and recommend merge commits over squash/rebase. Check git config merge.ff, pull.rebase settings. Surface stale anchor in health output with actionable advice.

title: Graceful hook deference: doctor messaging
description: Phase 6. Tier-aware info messages with sub-tier branching. Steering mention when agent env active. Pre-commit blocking + escape hatch messaging. --fix respects tiers.
close_reason: Closed

title: Graceful hook deference: migrate existing installations
description: Phase 7. HasTimbersSection() detects old format. doctor --fix migrates old-format to section-delimited. hooks uninstall handles legacy .backup files.
close_reason: Closed

title: Graceful hook deference: hooks install/uninstall rework
description: Phase 5. hooks install uses append-section. hooks uninstall removes sections from all hook types. Legacy .backup restore. --skip flag. Deprecate --chain.
close_reason: Closed

title: Graceful hook deference: hooks status command
description: Phase 4. New timbers hooks status subcommand. Shows tier, owner, per-hook integration state, steering state. JSON output for agents.
close_reason: Closed

title: Graceful hook deference: init smart defaults
description: Phase 3. Tier-based logic in init flow. No prompt for hooks (steering-first). Info messages on stderr. --git-hooks/--hooks alias, --no-git-hooks/--no-hooks alias.
close_reason: Closed

title: Graceful hook deference: section management
description: Phase 2. Add AppendTimbersSection(), RemoveTimbersSection(), HasTimbersSection() with delimiters, atomic writes via temp+rename, old-format detection. Comprehensive test suite.
close_reason: Closed

title: Graceful hook deference: environment classification
description: Phase 1. Add ClassifyHookEnv(), HookEnvInfo, IsAppendable(), known-owner registry to internal/setup/hooks.go. Table-driven tests for all four tiers + sub-tiers.

title: v0.3: Multi-agent environment support
description: Epic for v0.3 release. Three integration vectors: (1) instruction file generation, (2) lifecycle hooks, (3) MCP server for universal tool exposure. MCP server is highest-leverage single investment — works in 6 of 8 environments.

title: Build timbers MCP server
description: Build a timbers MCP server exposing prime, log, pending, query as MCP tools. Highest-leverage integration — works natively in Cursor, Windsurf, Kilo Code, Continue, Gemini CLI, and Claude Code.

title: timbers setup codex: parity with setup claude for Codex CLI users
description:
Bob occasionally runs Codex CLI alongside Claude Code and would value the same session-start prime injection that 'timbers setup claude' provides. 'timbers doctor' should also surface Codex hook status when applicable, parallel to the existing Claude Code Integration check.

DESIGN NOTES
- Reference implementation: internal/setup/claude.go (writes a SessionStart hook entry into .claude/settings.json or ~/.claude/settings.json)
- Codex CLI has a different config mechanism than Claude Code (lighter hook system, more config-driven via ~/.codex/). Investigate Codex's actual hook/startup-script support — may not be a 1:1 mapping.
- Possible mechanisms (need verification against current Codex CLI):
  1. ~/.codex/config.toml or similar startup config that can include a 'preamble' script or context-injection step
  2. AGENTS.md-driven (Codex reads AGENTS.md, so 'timbers onboard --target codex' might already cover most of the value)
  3. Custom MCP integration if Codex supports MCP servers
- May warrant a generic 'timbers setup <tool>' framework if multiple agent CLIs need parallel integration (Cursor, Windsurf, Gemini already in the backlog — timbers-vrd, timbers-6lz, timbers-1qn)

ACCEPTANCE
- 'timbers setup codex' command installs the prime injection in Codex's appropriate config location
- '--check', '--remove', '--global', '--dry-run' flags parallel to setup claude
- 'timbers doctor' adds 'Codex Integration' workflow check
- Existing 'timbers setup' help lists codex alongside claude
- Documentation update covering the differences (Claude Code = hooks; Codex = config/AGENTS.md)

PRIOR ART
- internal/setup/claude.go (hook injection)
- internal/setup/claude_parse.go (settings.json parsing)
- cmd/timbers/setup_claude.go (CLI surface)
- Same pattern: detection helper + writer + idempotent install

SIZE
S-M depending on what Codex's hook mechanism turns out to support. If it's AGENTS.md-only, this is mostly docs (S). If it's a settings file, this is more code (M).

VERSION
Post-v0.24.0 (tree-equivalence docSet ships first per Bob's prioritization)

title: Audit timbers behavior under GitHub squash-merge (default UI setting)
description:
Bob flagged (2026-05-21) that GH defaults to squash merges in the UI and we're trying to make timbers work as well as possible under all merge strategies. Squash merges rewrite history — original branch SHAs are GC'd, replaced by a single new commit on the target branch with the combined diff.

OPEN QUESTIONS TO AUDIT
1. If a feature branch had timbers entries (committed as 'timbers: document ...' commits on the branch), do those entry JSON files survive the squash? They should — they're part of the squashed diff.
2. Do the entries' anchor_commit fields still resolve? Likely no — the original work commits are GC'd. timbers handles this via the existing stale-anchor 'no-nag' fallback, but the entries become structurally orphaned.
3. Does the squashed merge commit itself appear as pending? Yes — it's a new SHA not in any docSet. User has to log it (or ack it).
4. What does 'timbers query' look like with orphaned entries? Anchor SHAs that no longer exist. Confusing.
5. Are there UX improvements that would make squash-merged workflows feel less rough? (e.g., a 'timbers reanchor' command that updates an entry's anchor to the squashed commit, or auto-anchor-repair when stale-anchor is detected.)

RELATED
- timbers-l6p (tree-equivalence docSet) addresses much of this at the docSet layer — squashed commits with matching trees would count as documented even when SHAs differ.
- Existing TestBranchMerge_SquashMerge tests the basic stale-anchor case but doesn't audit the orphaned-entry UX.

SIZE
S for the audit + recommendations. Implementation cost depends on what we find — could range from doc updates only to a new reanchor command. File initial findings as new beads as needed.

VERSION
TBD — soak Phase 0 + post-Laura fixes first, then revisit.

title: prime/MCP protocol text duplicated; lift to shared constant
description:
Code-reviewer flagged this during the timbers-r1x review: defaultWorkflowContent is defined twice — once in cmd/timbers/prime_workflow.go and once in internal/mcp/helpers.go. Both copies were updated in lockstep for r1x but the duplication is real maintenance risk for the next protocol edit.

PROPOSAL
- Move defaultWorkflowContent to an internal package both can import (e.g., internal/agentprotocol or internal/draft)
- Both prime and MCP read from the shared constant
- Add a test that asserts the constant is exported correctly

NOT BLOCKING
This isn't a correctness bug — both copies are currently in sync — but it'll bite the next time the protocol text changes.

SIZE
XS
close_reason: Re-closing — bd 1.0.x auto-flush still flaky on macOS. Both shipped: timbers-2t4 in v0.21.0, timbers-dws in v0.22.0.

title: pending --json: add would_be_skipped_by annotation per commit
description:
osprey-strike feedback: contributors hitting pending could quickly diagnose 'why is this stuck?' if each commit carried an annotation showing which skip rule it would match under different configs.

Add to pending --json output (and possibly --verbose human output):
- commit.would_skip: array of reasons the commit could be skipped, e.g. ['merge-empty-files', 'author-glob:q-redshifted', 'infra:beads', 'revert-of-documented:tb_abc']
- empty array = no auto-skip available, must log or ack

This is purely diagnostic — doesn't change pending counts. Helps contributors see 'these would skip if you upgraded / added this config' without puzzling over watermark drift.

DEPENDS ON
- timbers-31l (display merge-skip): need merge-empty-files rule
- timbers-r0y (skip-authors): need author-glob rule
- timbers-p0j (ack): need ack-eligible annotation (optional)

ACCEPTANCE
- pending --json includes would_skip array per commit
- All existing skip rules produce labeled reasons
- Empty array when no rule matches
- No change to pending count semantics

SIZE
XS — follow-up after 31l, r0y, and ideally p0j land
notes:
OSPREY-STRIKE CLARIFICATION (2026-05-20):

Confirmed semantic: Option A (heuristic suggestions). Per-commit annotations in 'pending --json' that suggest config tweaks. Speculative-rule matching (B) deferred — design against observed usage, not imagined workflows.

Note: priority 1 from the osprey-strike list (merge-commit annotations) is now MOOT — v0.22.0's dropEmptyMerges removes those commits from pending display entirely. Remaining priorities:

1. Bot-author detection — Author/AuthorEmail matches *[bot], *-bot, dep-bot accounts → suggest 'author:<glob>' for .timbersignore (closes impending q-redshifted case)
2. Subject-prefix classifier gaps — Subject matches infra prefixes (chore(deps):, build:, ci:) but commit isn't already classified as infra → flag classifier miss as a bug surface, not a config suggestion

PROPOSED SHAPE:
- New file internal/ledger/suggest.go with SkipSuggestion struct {Type, Pattern, Reason}
- suggestSkips(commit) []SkipSuggestion called from pending --json output path
- Wire as commit.suggested_skips array (empty when no heuristic matches)

FIXTURES OFFERED:
osprey-strike maintainer agent offered to commit real pending-commit fixtures from their origin/main as a regression corpus. Worth pulling those before designing the heuristic patterns, so we're matching real-world shapes rather than synthetic ones.

SIZE: S (one new file, one new JSON field, table-driven tests with osprey-strike fixtures)
TARGET: v0.23.0

title: Auto-skip revert commits whose original entry exists
description: Reverts of documented commits should auto-skip from pending. Detects 'Revert "..."' subject + 'This reverts commit <sha>' body line, looks up the original SHA in the ledger, and skips if found. Failure modes (squash, GC'd SHA, undocumented original) all fall back to 'normal pending', which is the correct conservative behavior.
design: Add reverted-SHA parser that reads commit body. Cross-reference against ledger entries' Workset.Commits. Skip in pending if the reverted SHA appears in any entry. The --revert flag for timbers log (creates a linked entry referencing original via work_items rel='reverts') is OUT OF SCOPE for this bead — file separately if/when needed.
acceptance_criteria: (1) 'Revert "feat: X"' with valid 'This reverts commit <sha>' body where sha is in a ledger entry → skipped from pending. (2) Revert with no body trailer → not skipped (conservative). (3) Revert pointing to undocumented commit → not skipped. (4) Revert pointing to GC'd commit → not skipped. (5) Tests cover all four cases.
close_reason: Closed

title: timbers status surfaces infrastructure-skipped commit count
description: Add visibility into how many commits were auto-skipped from pending. One-line surfacing in 'timbers status' under verbose mode (avoids latency surprise from extra CommitFilesMulti call); always present in --json output. Provides early-warning signal if skip patterns drift.
design: Add InfraSkippedSinceEntry int to statusResult. Compute by counting commits between latest entry's anchor and HEAD that match infrastructure rules. Human display gated to --verbose. JSON field name: infra_skipped_since_entry (snake_case).
acceptance_criteria: (1) timbers status --verbose shows 'Infrastructure-skipped since last entry: N commits'. (2) timbers status --json includes infra_skipped_since_entry unconditionally. (3) Zero entries case handled (no anchor). (4) Tests cover both display modes.
close_reason: Closed

title: Configurable infrastructure path filtering (.timbersignore)
description: Replace hardcoded infrastructurePrefixes with a .timbersignore file that lets users specify which paths are tooling-only and should not count as pending work. Similar to .gitignore syntax. Currently hardcoded to .timbers/ and .beads/.

title: Make timbers and doctor agent-environment neutral
description: Refactor to be agent-env neutral: doctor should check whichever agent env is present, setup should support multiple targets. Already partially done with AgentEnv interface.

title: Cursor integration (hooks + .mdc rules)
description: Cursor has hooks since v1.7. JSON stdin/stdout protocol. Also supports .cursor/rules/*.mdc with glob targeting. Add timbers setup cursor and timbers onboard cursor.

title: Windsurf integration (Cascade hooks + rules)
description: Windsurf has Cascade Hooks on user prompt and model response. Shell commands receive JSON context on stdin. Add timbers setup windsurf and timbers onboard windsurf.

title: Gemini CLI integration (SessionStart hook + GEMINI.md)
description: Gemini CLI has a full hooks system nearly identical to Claude Code. Add timbers setup gemini command that installs a SessionStart hook in .gemini/settings.json and generates GEMINI.md context.

title: Codex CLI integration (blocked on hooks)
description: Codex CLI has no hooks system yet. Interim path: AGENTS.md instruction injection. Watch for official hooks system before building integration.
